title: Odyssey, Book 9: The Cyclops
description: Book 9 of Homer's Odyssey: The Cyclops.
introduction: Book 9 of the Odyssey: The Cyclops. The Greek text follows the standard Homeric vulgate.

translation.text: Odysseus revealed his name and told his tale. After Troy, he raided the Cicones, was blown to the Lotus-Eaters, and came to the land of the Cyclopes. He entered the cave of Polyphemus, who ate six of his men. Odysseus got the Cyclops drunk on wine, blinded him with a burning stake, and escaped by clinging to the bellies of sheep. As he sailed away, he shouted his true name — and Polyphemus prayed to his father Poseidon for vengeance.
